prompt:
Combined follow-up to PR #76 (Settings move). Implements two filed issues plus a small cleanup. **Single PR, all changes together** â€” these touch the same files. ## Issue #74: Move Reset into Settings â†’ Data The Reset button currently lives in the Workouts bottom toolbar (`src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx`, around the reset-btn / handleReset). It calls `clearAll()` which wipes day count + exercise log + loaded program, gated by a single `confirm()` dialog. It's destructive and groups naturally with Export/Import. - Move `handleReset` from `src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx` into `src/tabs/Settings.tsx` - Add a Reset button to the existing **Data** card in Settings, visually separated from Export/Import with a divider or extra margin and a small "Danger zone" label using `text-flux-danger`. Suggested structure inside the Data card: ```tsx

Data

{/* Export + Import row stays */}
...
{importMessage && (...)} {/* New: Danger zone */}

Danger zone

``` - Remove the reset button from the Workouts bottom toolbar. After this, that toolbar holds only prev-day + next-day (and the flex spacer can be removed since there's nothing to push right). - Preserve `data-testid="reset-btn"`. The `confirm()` dialog stays unchanged. - **State refresh**: in Settings, `handleReset` should just call `clearAll()` and `setImportMessage('Reset complete.')` (or similar) â€” Settings doesn't hold workout state, so no setState/setLog/setProgram needed. When the user switches back to Workouts, the tab re-mounts and reloads from IDB via the existing effect (this is the same pattern PR #76 used for Import). ## Issue #75: Dismissible import status messages There are currently two import message displays â€” one in Workouts (program-import results, served by the existing `importMessage` state in `Workouts/index.tsx`) and one in Settings (backup-import results, added in PR #76). Both should get an inline dismiss control â€” a small Ă— button (or "Dismiss" link) inside the message card on the right side. Tapping it sets the relevant `importMessage` state to `null`. Suggested message-card shape (apply to BOTH locations): ```tsx {importMessage && (
{importMessage}
)} ``` ## Cleanup: disambiguate duplicate test IDs Both displays currently use `data-testid="import-message"` â€” a duplicate that should be split. Rename: - Workouts message: `data-testid="program-import-message"` (and dismiss â†’ `program-import-message-dismiss`) - Settings message: `data-testid="backup-import-message"` (and dismiss â†’ `backup-import-message-dismiss`) Update any tests that grep for `import-message` to use the new IDs. Run `grep -rn "import-message" tests/` to find all call sites and rewrite them according to which flow the test is exercising: - Tests for the program-import flow (look for `import-program` test IDs nearby, or context about loading a program JSON) â†’ `program-import-message` - Tests for the backup-import flow (look for `import-input` / `import-backup-label` nearby, or context about full backup restore) â†’ `backup-import-message` ## Add test coverage for dismiss Add at least one Playwright assertion verifying the dismiss flow â€” pick one of the import flows (whichever has the simplest fixture setup): 1. Trigger an import that sets a message 2. Assert the message is visible 3. Click the dismiss button (`getByTestId('-import-message-dismiss')`) 4. Assert the message is no longer in the DOM You can add this to whichever spec already exercises that flow (likely `tests/import-export.spec.ts` or `tests/import.spec.ts`). ## Validation (all must pass) - `nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test --project=chromium` â€” full e2e suite passes - `nix develop --command npm run typecheck` â€” no TS errors - `nix develop --command bash -c \"npm ci && npm run build\"` â€” production build succeeds ## Out of scope - Auto-dismiss timers (intentional â€” users should control when errors disappear) - Restyling the existing Settings sections (Appearance, LLM provider) - Touching the program-import button placement (just the message display) - Strengthening the Reset confirm dialog (separate concern; current `confirm()` is sufficient for now) - Adding new icons (use a plain `Ă—` text character for dismiss, no icon library) ## PR meta **Title:** "settings: move Reset to Data card; dismissible import messages" **Body:** brief description covering all three changes, closes #74 and closes #75, and one note acknowledging the Workouts bottom toolbar is now down to just prev-day + next-day after the Reset move.
